name: toast
description: Transient live-region status announcer (v-next). Three server-rendered JTE partials sharing one data model: toast-item (toast.jte) stamps a single notification card; toast/region.jte renders the two always-present live-region sub-containers (role=status polite for info/success, role=alert assertive for warning/destructive); notification-bell.jte is the icon-button + badge + popover history hub. The colocated toast.enhancer.ts owns queue management, countdown timers, auto-dismiss-pause-on-focus (react-aria useToast model), Esc-dismiss with focus restore, SSE insertion path, and bell counter. Variant vocabulary: info | success | warning | destructive. PARTIAL + ENH (ADR-0012); replaces the standalone toast.jte + viewport.
docs: Render toast/region.jte ONCE before </body> (outside any aria-modal surface). The two live-region sub-containers (polite + assertive) are always present at page load -- this is the load-order invariant for reliable AT announcements. Toasts arrive via: (1) the Lievit-Toast response header on any wire round-trip; (2) SSE when data-toast-sse-url is set on the region; (3) window.lievit.toast({...}) from adopter scripts. The enhancer manages the queue (maxVisible cap), countdown timers (paused while focused), Esc-dismiss with focus restore, and the notification-bell unread counter. Variant drives both the live-region sub-container (polite for info/success, assertive for warning/destructive) and the accent token pair. notification-bell.jte is optional: include it in your toolbar to give users a browsable notification history that accumulates across page load.
